questions.question: What is the primary goal of the 'Discovery Certification Challenge'?

options.text: To close a $1,000,000 deal.
To demonstrate mastery of discovery skills in a high-stakes, multi-stakeholder scenario.
To prove that you can talk longer than the AI.
To memorize 100 discovery questions.

questions.explanation: The certification challenge is a final assessment of your ability to apply all the techniques learned in the course to a realistic and difficult sales scenario.

questions.question: Which of these is worth the MOST points (35) in the certification scoring criteria?

options.text: Frame Management (Agenda setting)
Diagnostic Depth (PID Coverage—Pain, Impact, Decision)
Behavioral Composure (Talk-time ratio)
Strategic Recommendation (Go/No-Go decision)

questions.explanation: Uncovering the 'Truth' through a deep diagnosis (PID) is the most critical technical skill in discovery.

questions.question: In the certification scenario, if the AI Stakeholders are being 'Guarded,' what should you do in the first 10 minutes?

options.text: Push harder for pricing to prove you're serious.
Focus on building trust through empathy, industrial knowledge, and 'Permission' questions to earn the right to go deeper.
End the call immediately; they aren't interested.
Start a technical demo to show them the product's value.

questions.explanation: Earning trust is the only way to get a guarded prospect to reveal the information you need for a deep diagnosis.

questions.question: Why does the 'AI Board' award bonus points for 'Rapid, data-backed disqualification'?

options.text: Because they want you to talk to fewer people.
Because being willing to walk away from a bad fit proves you are a professional consultant prioritizing the 'Truth' over a quick sale.
Because they think disqualifying is easier than qualifying.
Because it makes the simulation shorter.

questions.explanation: Disqualification is a major survival skill for solo founders. It preserves your time for the deals that actually have a chance of closing.

questions.question: What is the 'Summary Defense' part of the certification process?

options.text: A list of reasons why the prospect should buy from you.
A 3-paragraph summary documenting Pain, Impact, and Decision data to justify your 'Pursue or Disqualify' decision.
An apology for any mistakes you made on the call.
A transcript of the entire 25-minute conversation.

questions.explanation: The summary defense proves that you didn't just 'have a good feeling,' but that you actually captured the data needed for a strategic business decision.

questions.question: Reflect on your journey through this course. What was the most significant 'Reflex' or 'Muscle Memory' you built? (e.g., waiting after a short answer, asking for impact, handling resistance). How will you ensure this reflex doesn't fade when you transition from the AI simulator to real-world discovery calls next week?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the student's progress and commitment to sustaining their new habits. Confirm they address the transition to the 'Real World.' Suggest one way they can use a 'Pre-Call Ritual' to keep their reflexes sharp.
